claim: Run /init on every project to auto-scan codebase and generate cloud.md file—acts as a reusable cheat sheet so Claude learns your architecture without re-explanation each session
actionable: Open any existing project in Claude Code and run /init; maintain cloud.md as a living document of your project patterns and conventions
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Keep cloud.md between 150-200 lines max by routing to separate files for style guides and reference docs—prevents bloating the system prompt that gets loaded every session
actionable: Link cloud.md to separate files instead of embedding everything inline; trim cloud.md ruthlessly to keep context window efficient
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Compact context at 60% fullness using /compact command; can selectively preserve constraints (e.g., 'keep API integration decisions') while shrinking conversation history
actionable: When context hits ~60%, run /compact with preservation instructions like '/compact, but keep database schema decisions'
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Plan mode (Shift+Tab) lets Claude outline approach without writing code; dramatically reduces revisions by forcing alignment before implementation
actionable: Start every complex task in plan mode; let Claude ask clarifying questions and map the approach, then switch to execute mode
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Use sub-agents for parallel work with isolated context windows; pair with Haiku for cheaper tokens on simple subtasks, keep main thread on Opus
actionable: When facing a complex problem, invoke sub-agents to research, write tests, or explore approaches in parallel; use Haiku for sub-agents, Opus for main
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Build custom skills in .cloud/skills directory as reusable prompt files; commit to GitHub so team can instantly use them as /slash commands
actionable: Create skill files like techdebt.md and codereview.md in .cloud/skills; reference them in prompts or /slash commands for reproducible workflows
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Use /loop for recurring tasks (e.g., 'check deployment every 5 minutes'); Claude reruns in same session without closing; limited to 3 days per loop
actionable: Set up /loop for monitoring tasks like deployment checks or log polling; use desktop scheduled tasks for longer-term automation
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Context7 MCP ensures Claude has live, version-specific documentation for libraries (React, Next.js, MongoDB, etc.); fixes stale training data problem
actionable: Install Context7 MCP once; Claude will fetch current docs before suggesting functions, preventing use of deprecated APIs
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Use /rewind to undo wrong turns without restarting; exit early and re-prompt if Claude goes off-track rather than wasting tokens on the wrong path
actionable: Monitor Claude's direction; hit Escape and re-prompt if veering wrong; use /rewind to backtrack instead of starting over
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Screenshots + Claude's vision enable self-check loops: build → screenshot → analyze → iterate 3 times before delivering V1 for much higher quality
actionable: Embed screenshot verification into to-do lists; have Claude check layout, then make changes, then screenshot again before handing to you
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Use Chrome DevTools integration to check app functionality, fill forms, handle interactive testing; Claude can interact with running applications
actionable: Invoke Chrome DevTools for front-end verification; let Claude click buttons, fill forms, check console errors in real-time
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Use git worktrees for parallel multi-agent sessions on same project without conflicts; each agent gets isolated branch, can merge later
actionable: Use 'Claude --worktree feature-name' to create isolated workspaces; run 3-5 agents on different branches simultaneously, merge when done
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Hardcode specific API endpoints instead of loading full MCP server definitions if only using one endpoint; saves massive context window tokens
actionable: For Notion-only read operations, hardcode the read endpoint instead of loading all Notion MCP tools
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Challenge outputs aggressively; if Claude gives mediocre code, reject it with 'Do a completely different approach' and it often gives dramatically better output on retry
actionable: Don't accept first attempts; push back hard; set a high bar and make Claude iterate until it's genuinely good
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Ultra think allocates 32K token thinking budget for hard architecture/debugging decisions; don't use for simple fixes, save for consequential calls
actionable: Use ultra think for system architecture decisions, complex refactors, or when Claude isn't delivering after 2 attempts
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins

claim: Edit permissions granularly: explicitly allow safe commands and deny destructive ones (delete, remove) instead of 'dangerously skip all'; maintains speed without danger
actionable: Configure .claude/settings.json with allowlist for safe commands (install, start) and denylist for destructive ones (rm, delete)
source_title: 32 Tricks to Level Up Claude Code in 16 Mins
